items.title: Energy drinks to be banned for under-16s in England from April
items.summary: It will be illegal to sell high-caffeine beverages to under-16s from April next year, but soft drinks with lower caffeine levels will not be affected.

items.title: Teenagers from 15 should be given free MenB vaccine, say UK experts
items.summary: Advisers are asking the government to consider introducing MenB jabs following concerns over an outbreak in Kent earlier this year, in which two people died.

items.title: Godinama izbjegavamo punomasno mlijeko, ali novo istraživanje donosi drukčiju priču
items.summary: PUNOMASNI maslac, sir i mlijeko godinama su na lošem glasu jer se često smatra da povećavaju rizik od srčanih bolesti, moždanog udara i dijabetesa.

items.summary: It follows the UK's largest and fastest growing meningitis B outbreak to date in Kent earlier this year.

items.title: Scientists finally solved how a common gut bacterium triggers colon cancer
items.summary: Researchers solved a long-standing mystery behind how a bacterial toxin associated with colorectal cancer damages the colon. items.title: More than 400 sick as CDC searches for the source of a mystery outbreak
items.summary: A growing Cyclospora outbreak has sickened more than 400 people in four states, and investigators are still searching for the contaminated food responsible. The CDC warns the actual number of cases is likely much higher and urges anyone with symptoms to seek medical care.

items.title: Common constipation drug may help clear depression brain fog
items.summary: An existing constipation drug may have an unexpected new use: helping clear the "brain fog" that often lingers after depression. items.title: 'I couldn't leave the house, I worried I'd cheat': The rise of relationship OCD
items.summary: Relationship OCD is gaining awareness, but limited data means little is known about how common the condition is.

items.title: Jesy Nelson calls plan to test newborns for life-limiting muscle condition a 'victory'
items.summary: All newborn babies are set to have a heel prick test for Spinal Muscular Atrophy (SMA) in England.
